_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to use host volatile data to compare active processes, libraries and modules against databases of known good/bad.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues that span multiple system layers, tracing problems across application code, data pipelines, databases, networks, and infrastructure to identify and remediate root causes.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-A-002 (DQ, cosine: 0.520), DQ-T-016 (DQ, cosine: 0.508).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.395). Shared vocabulary: [ability, data, databases]. DCWF-only terms [active, against, bad, compare, good, host] vs WIDAI-only terms [across, application, causes, code, diagnose, identify] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.855 (P=0.857, R=0.852). Balanced P/R — the 18-word DCWF element and 29-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.0%). "Ability to use host volatile data to compare active processes, libraries and mod" and "Ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues that span multiple system layer" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.537 > STS 0.395 (gap +0.143). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ability, data, databases] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.8%. Shared tokens: [ability, data, databases]. DCWF-only: [active, against, bad, compare, good, host]. WIDAI-only: [across, application, causes, code, diagnose, identify].
